name: wrike-automation
description: Automate Wrike project management via Rube MCP (Composio): create tasks/folders, manage projects, assign work, and track progress. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create, update, and delete tasks in Wrike workspaces
Manage folders and projects with full CRUD operations
Assign tasks to team members by user ID
Launch task blueprints to create tasks from templates
Retrieve and filter tasks by status, due date, or custom fields
Manage workspace members and send invitations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 80 static findings are false positives. The skill is a standard Wrike API integration via Rube MCP. External command detections are backticks used in markdown documentation to denote tool names (e.g., RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS), not actual shell execution. Network detection is a public service URL (rube.app/mcp), not sensitive data. The skill makes API calls to Wrike through the Rube MCP server, which is standard integration behavior.
valueStatement: Manually creating and managing Wrike tasks is time-consuming. This skill automates Wrike project management through Rube MCP, letting Claude create tasks, manage folders, assign work, and track progress via API.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Wrike OAuth
Cannot bypass Wrike permissions - operations depend on user role
Custom field IDs must be fetched before use - cannot guess IDs
Some features require paid Wrike plans (custom statuses, billing)

useCases.title: Project Manager Creates Sprint Tasks
useCases.description: Automatically create sprint tasks from a backlog list, assign to developers, and set due dates based on sprint timeline.

useCases.title: Team Lead Tracks Team Workload
useCases.description: Fetch all tasks for team members, filter by status and due dates, and generate workload reports for resource planning.

useCases.title: Operations Automates Recurring Tasks
useCases.description: Set up automated workflows to create tasks from templates using blueprints for recurring processes like weekly reports.

promptTemplates.title: Create a New Task
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a task in Wrike with title [TASK TITLE] in folder [FOLDER ID]. Set description to [DESCRIPTION], assign to [USER IDs], and set due date to [DATE].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to quickly create a task with standard parameters

promptTemplates.title: List Project Tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: Get all tasks from the [PROJECT/FOLDER NAME] project that are due this week. Show task title, assignee, status, and due date.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to review active tasks in a project

promptTemplates.title: Assign Work to Team
promptTemplates.prompt: Find the user IDs for [NAMES OR EMAILS] in the Wrike workspace, then assign task [TASK ID] to those users.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to assign or reassign tasks

promptTemplates.title: Launch Task Blueprint
promptTemplates.prompt: List available task blueprints in Wrike, then launch the [BLUEPRINT NAME] blueprint with title [TASK TITLE] in folder [FOLDER ID].
promptTemplates.scenario: When creating multiple tasks from a template

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Verify Rube connection status is ACTIVE before running any Wrike operations
Use folder ID resolution pattern to find correct folder/project IDs before creating tasks
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode folder or task IDs without first resolving them from list operations
Do not assume user IDs - always fetch contacts to get correct user identifiers
Do not skip checking connection status - operations will fail without active OAuth

faq.question: How do I connect Wrike to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client, then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'wrike' to complete OAuth.

faq.question: Can I create tasks without specifying a folder?
faq.answer: No. Wrike requires all tasks to belong to a folder. You must provide a folderId - find it using WRIKE_GET_FOLDERS first.

faq.question: How do I find user IDs for task assignment?
faq.answer: Call WRIKE_GET_CONTACTS to list all workspace members with their IDs. Use these IDs in the responsibles array.

faq.question: What is the difference between folders and projects?
faq.answer: Folders are containers for tasks. Projects are folders with additional project-specific fields. Create a folder with customItemTypeId to make it a project.

faq.question: Can I delete a folder with tasks in it?
faq.answer: Yes, but WRIKE_DELETE_FOLDER removes ALL contents permanently. Consider using MODIFY_FOLDER to move to recycle bin instead.

faq.question: Does this skill support custom fields?
faq.answer: Yes. Call WRIKE_GET_ALL_CUSTOM_FIELDS to get field definitions, then include {id, value} objects in the customFields parameter when creating tasks.
